name: Module 2 Review Mock — Moles, Redox & Electron Structure
note: Week 1 review mock. Covers HIGH-frequency spec points from Days 1–6. OCR Paper 1 shape: Section A (1-mark recall), Section B (2- and 3-mark structured). All questions original.

questions.question: State the value of the Avogadro constant, Nₐ, including its unit.
State the volume, in cm³, occupied by one mole of any ideal gas at RTP.
State the value of the gas constant R, including its unit.
State what is meant by the term 'empirical formula'.
State what happens to the oxidation number of an element when it is reduced.
State the oxidation number of oxygen in hydrogen peroxide (H₂O₂).
State on which side of an oxidation half-equation the electrons appear.
State the maximum number of electrons that can occupy a single atomic orbital.
State the shape of a 2s atomic orbital.
State the full electron configuration of potassium (K, Z = 19).
Calculate the amount, in mol, of sulfur dioxide (SO₂, M = 64.1 g mol⁻¹) in 9.62 g of the gas.
Calculate the volume, in dm³, occupied by 0.250 mol of nitrogen gas (N₂) at RTP.
A compound contains 75.0% carbon and 25.0% hydrogen by mass. Determine its empirical formula. [Ar: C = 12.0; H = 1.0]
Deduce the oxidation number of sulfur in each of the following: (i) H₂S; (ii) SO₃.
questions.context: Show your working in each case.

questions.question: Write the oxidation half-equation for aluminium being oxidised to Al³⁺ ions, and the reduction half-equation for hydrogen ions (H⁺) being reduced to hydrogen gas (H₂). Ensure each half-equation is balanced for atoms and charge.
Describe the 3p subshell in terms of the number of orbitals it contains and the maximum number of electrons it can hold.
Write the electron configurations of the iron atom (Fe, Z = 26) and the Fe³⁺ ion. State which electrons are removed first when the Fe³⁺ ion forms.
A sample of carbon dioxide gas has a volume of 750 cm³ at 57 °C and 200 kPa. Calculate the mass of CO₂ in this sample. [M(CO₂) = 44.0 g mol⁻¹; R = 8.314 J mol⁻¹ K⁻¹]
A hydrocarbon X contains 85.7% C by mass and has a relative molecular mass of 84.0. Determine the molecular formula of X. [Ar: C = 12.0; H = 1.0]

Chlorine reacts with cold, dilute sodium hydroxide to produce sodium chloride (NaCl) and sodium chlorate(I) (NaOCl). Explain why this reaction is an example of disproportionation, using oxidation numbers of chlorine in each product to support your answer.

Write the half-equations for the reaction between iron metal (Fe → Fe²⁺) and copper(II) ions (Cu²⁺ → Cu). Combine the two half-equations to give the overall ionic equation. State which species is the oxidising agent and justify your choice.

Explain why the electron configuration of copper (Z = 29) is [Ar] 3d¹⁰ 4s¹ rather than the expected [Ar] 3d⁹ 4s². Write the electron configuration of the Cu²⁺ ion and explain which electrons are removed.
